text: The NCCM system shall require an employee to enter their user name and password for access.
The system shall provide two levels of access: one for teachers and assistants and one for administrators.
Teachers and assistants shall only be allowed to enter or edit child comments and child arrival or departure times.
Administrators shall be allowed to enter or edit all data.
Only administrators shall be allowed to view or print reports.
Only administrators shall be allowed to view or print invoices.
The system shall allow all employees to use the Daily Reminders feature.
The system shall require each user to log on with a unique user name.
The system shall assign a password to each user.
The password shall contain 6 to 8 alphanumeric characters.
The system shall lock out a user after three unsuccessful password attempts.
The system shall assign a new password if a user forgets their current one.
The system shall log off the user if there is no activity for 10 minutes.
The system shall allow users to set daily reminders only for themselves.
The system shall provide a calendar for selecting the date of a reminder.
The system shall allow the user to enter a reminder message up to 50 characters.
The system shall display a popup window with the reminder message upon user login on the specified date.
A child record shall contain the dates that their required immunizations were received.
The system shall display a message when a teacher logs on if a child is within two weeks of a required immunization.
A child record shall contain the date of their last physical examination.
Administrators shall be able to enter the type and schedule of required immunizations.
The system shall print a notification for required immunizations on the monthly invoice.
The system shall bill a parent $90 at the end of each month if only a single child is enrolled.
The system shall bill a parent $157.50 at the end of each month if only two children are enrolled.
The system shall bill a parent $157.50 for the first two children plus $45 for each additional child if more than two are enrolled.
The system shall maintain an account history for each customer containing all activity by date.
The system shall provide itemized customer invoices for printing.
The system shall bill customers $10 an hour per child for late pick ups.
The system shall make all invoices available for printing at the end of each month.
The Customer Directory Report shall list customers alphabetically by last name and include address and phone number.
The Daily Classroom & Center Enrollment Report shall list children alphabetically by classroom and indicate presence or absence.
The Birthday Lists & Child Age Report shall list children alphabetically by last name with their birthday and age.
The Parent/Child Cross Reference Report shall list parents alphabetically by last name with their children's first names.
The Family Registration Information Report shall list customer names alphabetically with address and home telephone number.
The Immunization Due & History Report shall list children alphabetically with required remaining immunizations and previous dates.
The Child Enrollment/Withdrawal Report shall list children alphabetically and their registration status.
The Child Notes & Comments Report shall list children alphabetically with attached notes.
The Account Activity Report shall list a selected customer's account activity by date.
The system shall record the user ID, date, time, and description of change when an account is edited.
The Parent record shall contain name, address, home phone, work phone, email, emergency contact, and authorized pick up.
The Child record shall contain name, date of birth, gender, classroom name, special needs, photo, teacher comments, and immunization dates.
The software shall support the entry, storage, and update of parent, child, and billing information.
Customer log notes shall store the date and comments regarding correspondence with a parent.
Each classroom shall accept a maximum of 20 children.
Each student shall belong to only one class.
The system shall assign one teacher and one assistant to each class.
The waiting list shall store the names and phone numbers of up to 100 potential customers.
The system shall provide a Web based interface compatible with Internet Explorer and Netscape Navigator.
The background color of all windows shall be blue.
The system shall respond to all user requests within 20 seconds.
The NCCM system must be hosted on a Web server supporting Microsoft ASP.NET technology.
